steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The second team hypothesizes that fish sensors are most sensitive exactly where information (pressure difference) is greatest. The study established that for narrower heads, peak pressure difference is closer to the snout; for wider heads, it is farther from the snout. A supporting finding must show sensor sensitivity matching these locations for two fish with different head widths.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "The longnose gar (*Lepisosteus osseus*) has a much narrower head than the devil catfish (*Bagarius bagarius*), and the most sensitive sensors of the longnose gar are closer to the snout than are those of the devil catfish.": narrower head $\Rightarrow$ sensors closer to snout; wider head $\Rightarrow$ sensors farther from snout. This matches the hypothesis perfectly for both species. ✓
  • "The longnose gar (*Lepisosteus osseus*) has a much narrower head than the devil catfish (*Bagarius bagarius*), and the most sensitive sensors for both are very close to their snouts.": places the devil catfish's most sensitive sensors near the snout, but the hypothesis predicts they should be farther from the snout for a wider-headed fish. ✗
  • "The longnose gar (*Lepisosteus osseus*) has a much narrower head than the devil catfish (*Bagarius bagarius*), and while the sensors nearest the snout for the longnose gar are more sensitive than the others, for the devil catfish all sensors are equally sensitive.": if all sensors are equally sensitive, there is no location where sensitivity peaks — this contradicts the hypothesis that sensitivity tracks the location of greatest information. ✗
  • "The longnose gar (*Lepisosteus osseus*) has a much narrower head than the devil catfish (*Bagarius bagarius*), and the most sensitive sensors for the devil catfish are closer to the snout than are those for the longnose gar.": this reverses the predicted pattern; the wider-headed species should have its most sensitive sensors farther from the snout, not closer. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
